articles.title: EU Unlocks $106 Billion Loan for Ukraine — Here's What Took So Long
articles.summary: Ukraine just secured a massive $106 billion loan from the European Union, a crucial lifeline that could cover two-thirds of its funding needs for the next two years. Without it, officials warned Ukraine might run out of money to pay its soldiers and keep basic services running as early as this spring. The deal was held up for months because Hungary's outgoing prime minister, Viktor Orbán — widely seen as Russia's closest ally in the EU — blocked it. After Orbán lost an election, the logjam broke, but only after Ukraine repaired a key oil pipeline that supplies Hungary and Slovakia. About a third of the funds will go to Ukraine's government budget, while the rest will be spent on…

articles.title: Appeals Court Strikes Down Trump's Asylum Ban — What It Means for Migrants
articles.summary: A federal appeals court just ruled that President Trump's executive order suspending asylum at the southern border is illegal. A three-judge panel from the D.C. Circuit Court found that U.S. immigration laws clearly give people the right to apply for asylum, and the president can't override that by declaring an "invasion." Trump had issued the order on Inauguration Day 2025, saying he was suspending the physical entry of migrants until he decided the crisis was over. The court said the law doesn't give the president that kind of power. The White House says it will appeal, possibly to the Supreme Court. Advocates call the ruling a crucial victory for people fleeing war and persecution. But the ruling doesn't take effect…

articles.title: US Sends Envoys to Pakistan as Iran's Top Diplomat Arrives — Will They Talk?
articles.summary: The United States is sending envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan as Iran's foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, arrives in the country, raising hopes for new talks on ending the US-Israeli war on Iran. But it's far from certain. Iran hasn't committed to meeting the Americans, and Araghchi's public statements only mention "bilateral matters" with Pakistan, Oman, and Russia. The talks would focus on a fragile ceasefire and tensions over the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial waterway for global oil shipments. The US has been applying pressure, including freezing $344 million in Iranian cryptocurrency assets. Two Pakistani sources say Araghchi's visit will be brief, focused on conveying Iran's proposals through Pakistan as a mediator. The White House says it's…
